mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at a low absolute level relative to its trailing 12-month average signals ongoing monetary accommodation, lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following low-rate periods are not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that leads investors to downgrade earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the sign of the response is unstable across at least two distinct historical business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects a broad-based consumer and residential investment expansion that jointly signals strengthening aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither series alone captures this synergy.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to produce forward 20d returns exceeding the unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.10), or single-series models achieve equal or higher Rank IC than the joint interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, providing a short-horizon leading signal for equity risk appetite that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, indicating no persistent short-horizon predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth crosses from contraction to two consecutive months of positive month-over-month gains during an early-cycle recovery, it signals a durable turn in labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS posts two consecutive months of positive m/m change after prior contraction
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following the trigger are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the signal fails to replicate across at least two separate recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation combined with a federal funds rate that remains below the inflation rate (implying negative real rates) signals a monetary regime where inflation risk premia rise faster than nominal policy tightening, causing equity valuations to compress and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this mechanism requires the joint condition and does not hold if either series moves in isolation.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL y/y change > FEDFUNDS level (negative real rate) for 2+ consecutive months
failure_condition: Negative real-rate episodes produce positive forward 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction model shows no improvement in Rank IC over the FEDFUNDS-only or CPIAUCSL-only single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals an emerging contraction in aggregate output that is expected to translate into weaker corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 q/q annualized growth turns negative
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d forward returns in more than 50% of quarters following a negative GDPC1 print, or the relationship disappears once controlling for revision noise inherent in the high-revision-risk GDPC1 series.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-led recovery regime, a sustained rise in housing starts (HOUST) above its trailing 6-month average signals renewed residential investment activity that supports broader consumer confidence and construction-linked employment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; this effect is expected to be strongest specifically in post-recession recovery periods and muted in mature expansions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 10% above its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: The positive return effect fails to appear or reverses sign when tested in mature-expansion subsamples, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window during the recovery regime.
